params.prompt: Transform this room image into a cinematic editorial real-estate still. Preserve exact architecture, perspective, walls, windows, door openings, furniture layout, lens realism, and room scale. Maintain believable residential scale for a 7.833 by 8 ft Dine. Day: strong bay-window light; the oversized woven pendant acts as evening focal light (warm amber). Use pendant practical as visible light source in evening frames—it should cast soft patterned shadows on the table. Avoid introducing additional visible overheads. A calm, warm Japandi interior that balances Japanese minimal restraint with Scandinavian warmth. Prioritize low-profile furniture, natural materials (light oak, walnut, jute), layered textures, and a single cozy work corner for a young family. Keep decoration minimal and purposeful: 2–3 ceramics, one sculptural plant cluster, and a woven pendant as focal punctuation above the dining table. Keep these fixed unless the user explicitly wants transformation: exact architecture room geometry furniture layout lens realism vertical line integrity believable scale light entering from visible sources only VISUAL CONSISTENCY REQUIREMENT (reference image provided): The dining table, pendant light above the dining area, dining chairs, round light-oak dining table — solid light oak, 48-inch diameter, slim tapered legs, natural oiled finish, oversized woven pendant — oversized circular woven rattan pendant, natural straw color, 24-inch diameter, suspended matte black canopy in this room must exactly match the style, materials, and form shown in reference image — treat it as the definitive source of truth for these pieces. The Taste visible through the open kitchen must show exactly the same kitchen cabinets, range hood, kitchen island or counter, round light-oak dining table — solid light oak, 48-inch diameter, slim tapered legs, natural oiled finish, matte black hardware accents as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. The Nest visible through the open-plan living area must show exactly the same sofa, coffee table, area rug, low-profile 3-seater sofa — pale oat linen upholstery, dark walnut tapered legs, low 18" seat height, chaise-free symmetrical form, large woven area rug — handwoven jute in warm sand (#EDE6DE), 8x10, flatweave with subtle chevron texture, compact standing desk — warm ash veneer top, matte black steel legs, 48" width, integrated cable channel, minimal apron, organic low coffee table — oval solid walnut coffee table, matte oil finish, rounded edges, 36in x 20in, low 12" height, bookshelf as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. Previous failure modes to correct: object_missing, geometry_warp. The image showcases a well-lit dining area with a natural aesthetic, suitable for a Japandi style. However, the round dining table does not appear to match the specified dimensions and finish (48-inch diameter, slim tapered legs, natural oiled finish), which affects object completeness. Additionally, furniture items in sightlines may confuse the room identity, leading to room correctness issues. Overall, reshooting with focus on the required specifications is recommended.
